themes.name: Customer Support & Issue Resolution
themes.description: Users are frequently reporting issues with customer support, including unresponsiveness, closed tickets without resolution, difficulty in contacting support, and unresolved financial transactions.

themes.name: Charges & Fees Transparency
themes.description: Users are expressing dissatisfaction with hidden charges, unexpected brokerage fees, and unclear fee structures, particularly for US stocks and certain transactions.

themes.name: App Functionality & User Experience
themes.description: Reviews highlight issues with app updates negatively impacting usability, problems with specific features like portfolio tracking, UPI payments, and order modification, as well as difficulties with KYC processes.

themes.name: Investment Features & Data Analysis
themes.description: Positive feedback is given for the app's ability to provide data for fund analysis and comparison, and for its US stock investment features. However, some users request improvements in portfolio section complexity and manual tracking options.

themes.name: Account & Transaction Issues
themes.description: Users are reporting problems with failed payments, money not being credited or refunded, and accounts being closed without explanation or balance refund.

quotes.text: worst customer support indmoney has.. pathetic.. I want to close my account they aren't helping.

Warning ⚠️ I bought 15 shares ranging from ₹1 to ₹25 and when I sold them they charged DP Charges of ₹14+18% GST on every Share I sold. My wallet balance got in Negative. The problem is that They won't tell you all this before adding funds in your Demat wallet. Everything is hidden beneath FAQ pages if you dig. Invest in Mutual funds to avoid all hidden charges.
Finally, an app that gives a real, consolidated view of my net worth. The automatic tracking is incredibly efficient

weekly_pulse.note: User feedback over the past 10 weeks indicates significant pain points around customer support responsiveness and issue resolution, with many users reporting unresolved financial transactions and closed tickets. Transparency around charges and fees, especially for US stocks, remains a concern, leading to user frustration and distrust. While the app's core functionality for investment analysis and US stock trading is appreciated, recent updates have negatively impacted user experience, with issues in portfolio tracking, UPI payments, and KYC processes. Addressing these core areas is crucial for improving user satisfaction and retention.
actions: Implement a tiered customer support system with clear SLAs for ticket resolution, prioritizing financial transaction issues and ensuring no tickets are closed without explicit user confirmation of resolution.
Conduct a thorough review of all fee structures, particularly for US stocks and low-value trades, and ensure all charges are prominently displayed within the app before a transaction is confirmed.

Prioritize fixing the reported issues with the recent app update, specifically focusing on the portfolio refresh mechanism, UPI payment failures, and the KYC process, and consider a rollback or phased rollout for problematic features.

fee_scenario: Mutual Fund Exit Load on INDmoney Platform
explanation_bullets: An exit load is a charge levied by a mutual fund house when an investor redeems their units before a specified period.
The exit load percentage and the lock-in period for its applicability are determined by the Asset Management Company (AMC) of the specific mutual fund scheme.
INDmoney, as a platform, facilitates the redemption process and displays the applicable exit load as per the fund's scheme information document.
If units are redeemed within the stipulated exit load period, the calculated exit load amount will be deducted from the redemption proceeds.
The exit load is typically a percentage of the Net Asset Value (NAV) at the time of redemption.
